economic_notes: Enoch Cree Nation is the Host First Nation of the River Cree Resort and Casino and receives 30% of slot revenue. Entities include River Cree Resort, casino operations, gas bar, cannabis shop, construction, utilities, and land development. Nation advanced $20,323,254 in First Nations Development Funds to River Cree Resort entities for operating and debt servicing. Yekau Lake specific claims settlement of $91,000,000 received in prior years managed in trust.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Complex consolidation of 32 fully consolidated subsidiaries and 12 entities on modified equity basis including River Cree Resort casino operations. First Nations Finance Authority financing of $299,711,790 is a major liability used to fund advances to Enoch Cree Nation Business Trust ($256,303,381 due from trust). Loss from River Cree Resort investment of $14,265,406 recorded. New accounting standards adopted April 1, 2022 (PS 3450 Financial Instruments, PS 3041 Portfolio Investments, PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations, PS 1201 Financial Statement Presentation) resulting in remeasurement losses of $1,034,788. Restricted cash includes Yekau Lake Trust ($55,090,899) and Settlement Trust Funds ($6,073,052). Total government transfers calculated as ISC $35,452,761 + CMHC $1,285,458 + ISETS $957,154 + Government of Alberta $534,124 + FNDF $24,488,761 + FNDF River Cree Resort $20,323,255 = $82,946,513 less loss on River Cree investment which is not a government transfer. OSR includes taxation, business enterprises, resource/investment income, Mechet Charities, and other revenue.
